Title: Engineering the stereoisomeric structure of seed oil to mimic human milk fat
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: sn-2-palmitate-enriched triacylglycerol (human milk fat substitute-like TAG)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves infant fat and calcium absorption and reduces formation of palmitate soaps when used as human milk fat substitute
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PDCT increases retention/incorporation of C16:0 at the sn-2 position of seed TAG; in combination with ΔCTS-LPAT1 and lpat2-3 enables >70% sn-2 C16:0
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis using pdct (rod1) crossed with ProGLY:ΔCTS-LPAT1; TAG purification and positional analysis by lipase digestion; HR/AM lipidomics
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Disruption of PDCT increased C16:0 at the sn-2 position of TAG from ~30% in ProGLY:ΔCTS-LPAT1 to ~56% in the pdct background. 2. Combining pdct with lpat2-3 and ΔCTS-LPAT1 raised sn-2 C16:0 to ~71%, comparable to human milk fat. 3. The effect is interpreted as promoting direct flux of newly made DAG into TAG and bypassing PC-mediated acyl editing.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard laboratory growth conditions; seed development
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockout/suppression of PDCT can be combined with C16:0-selective sn-2 acyltransferase engineering to produce plant oils with human milk fat-like stereoisomeric structure
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contributes to reduced seed oil content when combined with ΔCTS-LPAT1 and lpat2-3; effect may vary across oilseed species due to interspecific variation in DAG-PC flux
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PDCT is treated here as a common gene because it redirects flux between DAG and PC rather than being the focal catalytic determinant for terminal nutrient synthesis; the paper uses the pdct mutant previously described as reduced oleate desaturation1

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: sn-2-palmitate-enriched triacylglycerol (human milk fat substitute-like TAG)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves infant fat and calcium absorption and reduces formation of palmitate soaps when used as human milk fat substitute
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced LPAT2 expression relieves competition with ΔCTS-LPAT1 and enhances C16:0 incorporation at the sn-2 position of TAG
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Isolation of promoter-region T-DNA alleles lpat2-2 and lpat2-3; qRT-PCR of developing siliques; genetic crosses with ProGLY:ΔCTS-LPAT1; TAG positional analysis by lipase digestion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The hypomorphic lpat2-3 allele reduced LPAT2 transcript abundance by ~83% in developing siliques. 2. In the ΔCTS-LPAT1 background, lpat2-3 increased sn-2 C16:0 in TAG from ~33% to ~51%, while weaker lpat2-2 repression had no significant effect. 3. LPAT2 therefore competes with ΔCTS-LPAT1 during seed TAG biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard laboratory growth conditions; seed development
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial repression of endogenous LPAT2 is a useful strategy to reduce competition and improve engineering of sn-2 palmitate-rich seed oil
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: LPAT2 is essential; null mutant is embryo lethal, so only partial suppression/hypomorphic alleles are viable; combined engineering reduced total seed oil content
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Effect on sn-2 palmitate enrichment scales with strength of LPAT2 repression; greater than ~83% reduction might further improve enrichment but may risk viability

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: sn-2-palmitate-enriched triacylglycerol (human milk fat substitute-like TAG)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Human milk fat substitute-like TAG is beneficial for infant nutrition by improving absorption of palmitate and calcium and reducing intestinal soap formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific expression of ER-retargeted ΔCTS-LPAT1 drives strong incorporation of C16:0 into the sn-2 position of TAG, increasing it from ~2% in wild type to ~32–39% alone and to ~71% when combined with lpat2-3 and pdct
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular relocalization by transient expression and confocal microscopy in Nicotiana benthamiana; transgenic seed-specific expression in Arabidopsis; TAG purification and lipase positional analysis; HR/AM lipidomics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of the chloroplast targeting signal retargeted B. napus LPAT1 to the ER. 2. Seed-specific ΔCTS-LPAT1 expression in Arabidopsis increased C16:0 at the TAG sn-2 position more than 16-fold to ~32–39%. 3. Combining ΔCTS-LPAT1 with lpat2-3 and pdct raised sn-2 C16:0 to ~71%, mimicking human milk fat.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Membrane-bound lysophosphatidic acid acyltransferase; plastidial LPAT lacking N-terminal chloroplast targeting signal
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum after CTS deletion; chloroplast in native form
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific transgene expression in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development under standard laboratory growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key engineering module for creating vegetable oils that mimic human milk fat; should be stacked with suppression of endogenous LPAT2 and PDCT to maximize sn-2 palmitate enrichment in oilseed crops
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alone insufficient for >70% sn-2 palmitate; combined engineering reduced seed oil content but did not significantly impair germination or seedling establishment under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The applied species is Arabidopsis, but the engineered LPAT1 coding sequence was from Brassica napus; localization and substrate access were altered by removing the chloroplast transit/targeting sequence
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: phosphatidic acid containing C16:0 at the sn-2 position
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for determining acyl composition at the sn-2 position of TAG; engineered determinant of stereoisomeric structure
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates the sn-2 position of lysophosphatidic acid using palmitoyl donor, thereby introducing C16:0 into phosphatidic acid that feeds TAG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream to midstream acylation step in glycerolipid/TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly establishes the sn-2 acyl group that is retained in the target TAG product
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triacylglycerol biosynthesis; cytosolic glycerolipid biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ER-localized de novo glycerolipid/TAG assembly pathway
